nuggets.headline: After 'OK Computer,' Radiohead abandoned their label to release 'In Rainbows' as 'pay-what-you-want.'
nuggets.text: Radiohead isn't just known for their innovative sound; they're also trailblazers in how they do business. After their landmark album *OK Computer* solidified their status, they consciously moved away from traditional industry practices. They left their longtime label, EMI, and famously released *In Rainbows* directly to fans as a 'pay-what-you-want' digital download, a move that redefined how artists could connect with their audience.

source.title: Radiohead: The Inside Story
source.quoteSnippet: Radiohead's approach to business since OK Computer is a significant source of their mystique... left their label, EMI, after delivering Hail to the Thief (2003)... In Rainbows (2007) was released on their own as a pay-what-you-want digital download.

nuggets.imageCaption: Radiohead, known for unconventional music and business practices.

nuggets.headline: The 'weird fishes' in the lyrics symbolize irresistible temptations you might try to escape.
nuggets.text: As these intricate arpeggios swirl around you, notice how Thom Yorke sings of 'weird fishes.' These aren't just random images; they're often interpreted as symbols of strong temptations that pull you in, even when you know they might lead you astray. The feeling of being drawn in, then realizing you need to hit rock bottom to escape, is a profound journey of self-preservation that plays out through the song's ever-present musical currents.

source.title: The meaning of Weird Fishes Arpeggi
source.quoteSnippet: The 'weird fishes' are interpreted as symbols for enticement and temptation... Yorke is drawn to these 'weird fishes'... The lyrics also speak about Yorke hitting the bottom and escaping... a metaphor for hitting rock bottom in life and realizing it's time for an ascent.

nuggets.headline: If you love the sweeping, melancholic drama of 'Weird Fishes,' try Muse's earlier work.
nuggets.text: If the layered, dramatic, and emotionally charged sound of 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' really grabs you, you should check out the band Muse, especially their albums from the early to mid-2000s. They often create epic, almost theatrical rock songs with intricate guitar lines and powerful, high-ranging vocals that share that same sense of beautiful melancholy and grand scope you hear in this track.
nuggets.imageCaption: Muse creates dramatic rock with intricate guitar and soaring vocals.

artistSummary: Radiohead consistently defies expectations, evolving from an Abingdon school band into rock pioneers. They challenge their own sound and industry conventions, crafting complex music that invites deep analysis.
